title: Announcing the 2026 Global Water Awards Winners
author: Global Water Intelligence
description: Global Water Intelligence announced the winners of the 2026 Global Water Awards during the Global Water Summit on May 19, 2026. The awards recognized excellence across expanded categories including new awards for AI Project of the Year, Utility of the Year, and separate categories for Industrial and Municipal Reuse Projects. Winners included Xylem (Water Company of the Year), Acwa (Desalination Company of the Year), and various utilities and projects across water infrastructure, desalination, wastewater treatment, and water reuse.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Won Water Company of the Year award, recognizing it as the industry's leading water company

title: Leak Detection Market Size to Hit USD 8.53 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The U.S. leak detection market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 5.31% through 2035, driven primarily by regulatory compliance requirements and aging pipeline infrastructure. Handheld detectors dominate the market with 47% share, while UAV-based detectors represent the fastest-growing segment. North America leads globally with 48% market share, while Asia Pacific shows the strongest growth potential at 7.48% CAGR.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company launched next-generation BACnet-connected gas detection platform in 2026 with AI-powered algorithms reducing false alarms by 60%, demonstrating technological advancement and market leadership in the growing leak detection sector.
Listed as a leading market player but no specific recent developments or market activities mentioned in the article.
Listed as a leading market player through Waygate Technologies subsidiary but no specific recent developments or market activities mentioned in the article.
Listed as a leading market player but no specific recent developments or market activities mentioned in the article.

Deployed AI-based leak detection system across major European refining sites with long-term service agreements, showing strong market penetration and regulatory compliance capabilities aligned with EU Methane Regulation requirements.

title: Is This Sector the Hidden Bottleneck of the AI Boom? (Hint: It's Not Semiconductors.)
author: Brett Schafer
description: AI data centers require significant water for cooling, creating opportunities for water infrastructure companies. Xylem and American Water Works are positioned to benefit from increased water demand, though investors should be cautious as data centers represent only a small portion of overall water usage and potential AI bubble risks exist.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While positioned to benefit from data center water cooling demand with growing net orders (+9% YoY), the stock trades at a high P/E of 32.6 with historically slow revenue growth. The article cautions that data centers are only a small segment of water users, limiting upside potential.

Benefits from regulated utility model and consistent dividend growth history, with recent merger expansion. However, the article notes that data center water usage is a small portion of total demand, and AI investment sustainability is uncertain, making it unlikely to be a significant growth driver.

title: Global Water Awards 2026: Voting Opens as Full Shortlist Is Revealed
author: Global Water Intelligence
description: Global Water Intelligence opens voting for the 2026 Global Water Awards across 14 categories, introducing a new AI Project of the Year category. The awards recognize excellence in the international water sector for 2025. Meanwhile, GWI DesalData reports that Middle East desalination projects are expected to add over 10 million cubic meters per day of capacity by 2035 worth $21 billion, despite regional conflicts, though Iran faces potential water infrastructure crisis.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nominated for Water Company of the Year, reflecting industry recognition for significant contributions to the water sector.
Recognized for water stewardship contributions to nature and communities, demonstrating corporate responsibility in water management.
Recognized for water stewardship contributions, highlighting commitment to sustainable water practices.
Recognized for water stewardship contributions through Nestlé Waters & Premium Beverages division, demonstrating corporate water sustainability efforts.
Nominated for AI Project of the Year for its AI-driven asset management programme for France's largest drinking water network, demonstrating innovation and recognition in the water sector.
Shortlisted for Water Company of the Year award, indicating peer recognition for excellence and contribution to the international water sector.

title: Dewatering and Membrane Future Technology Research Report 2026: Mapping High, Medium, and Low-impact Innovations Across Advanced Filtration, Tailings Water Recovery, and Process-water Reuse.
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: A comprehensive research report analyzing high, medium, and low-impact innovations in dewatering and membrane technologies for mining and heavy industry. The report examines advanced filtration, tailings water recovery, and process-water reuse technologies, using patent analytics, commercialization signals, and investment activity to assess technology maturity and competitive positioning in the shift toward closed-loop water systems.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Water technology company with expertise in filtration and dewatering systems; included among key innovators addressing water efficiency and recovery challenges in mining and heavy industry.
Global environmental services company specializing in water management and treatment solutions; featured as innovator in dewatering and membrane technologies for industrial applications.
